action.hypothesis: The explored nonlinear, mixture, and thresholded families are all well behind the K=128 TopK anchor, so the next clean probe is `gated`, which changes selection dynamics by separating gate and magnitude branches and could improve active-feature choice without requiring a brand-new codepath.
action.summary: Run a proxy `gated` experiment at K=128 with the same Adam/LR settings as the current TopK anchor to test whether decoupling selection and magnitude can materially improve reconstruction quality.
action.self_review: This is still a first-pass family check, and `gated` may simply inherit the same quality ceiling as plain TopK while adding optimization complexity. If it lands near the other failed prototypes, that strengthens the case for returning to TopK-focused quality work or implementing a genuinely new family.
notes_to_memory: `jumprelu` at default hyperparameters matched the weak proxy band of other alternative families and did not show a plausible path below the K=128 TopK anchor.

The architecture search so far suggests that modest encoder expressivity changes are not enough; remaining probes should either be qualitatively different or push back toward stronger TopK-family quality interventions.
next_hypotheses: If `gated` is competitive in proxy, promote it or tune its optimization settings before broader conclusions.

If `gated` is also clearly worse than TopK, stop cycling through near-neighbor families and either try a stronger TopK-quality regularization pass or implement a genuinely new routed/multi-branch family.
If `gated` is quality-promising but slower, keep it incubating and follow with a targeted performance pass.
